phase1: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. ⏰ SESSION & DATA RELEASE STATUS CHECK (MANDATORY FIRST STEP — complete this before any other analysis): (1) List ALL major US, European, and Asian macro data releases and central-bank events scheduled for today [CURRENT_DATE] (include exact release times in UTC). (2) For each release, determine whether it has ALREADY OCCURRED (post-data) or is still PENDING (pre-data). (3) Output a clear status line: If pending: 'PRE-DATA BIAS — [Event Name] pending at [HH:MM UTC] — bias carries LOWER CONFIDENCE until release. Reduce final Phase 6 confidence by at least 1 point per pending Tier-1 event (NFP, CPI, FOMC).' If all released: 'POST-DATA BIAS — All major releases confirmed. Standard confidence scoring applies.' This status line MUST be referenced and reflected in the Phase 6 Confidence score. Output: - Scheduled Events Today: [list each event + release time UTC + status: OCCURRED / PENDING] - Session Status: [PRE-DATA / POST-DATA / MIXED] | Confidence Adjustment: [state penalty if any]  Browse the live web for the latest Federal Reserve policy and liquidity data. Fetch and report: - Fed current stance (Hawkish / Dovish / Neutral) and the official Fed Funds Rate target range - CME FedWatch: exact rate-cut probability (%) for the next FOMC meeting — state the meeting date - CME FedWatch year-end Fed Funds rate strip — where is the market pricing the terminal rate by year-end? (exact %) - Quantitative Tightening (QT) status: is QT still active? Current monthly balance sheet run-off pace (USD billions). Has there been any taper or pause announcement? - Fed Balance Sheet total size — current level (USD trillions) and recent trend (expanding/contracting) - Any Fed speaker comments in the last 48 hours (name + hawkish/dovish tone) - Why this matters: Tech stocks and the US100 are highly sensitive to liquidity conditions. Rate-cut expectations reduce the discount rate on future earnings (Bullish for growth stocks). QT drains liquidity and raises real yields (Bearish for tech multiples). ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - Fed Stance: [Hawkish/Dovish/Neutral] | Fed Funds Rate: [X%–X%] | Next Meeting: [date] - Cut Probability Next Meeting: [X%] | Year-End Terminal Rate: [X%] - QT Status: [Active/Paused/Ended] | Monthly Run-Off: [USD Xbn] | Balance Sheet: [USD Xtrn, Expanding/Contracting] - Key Fed Speaker (last 48h): [name + tone + key quote or None] - US100 Policy Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P1 - FED POLICY & LIQUIDITY] - Fed Stance: [stance + Fed Funds Rate range] - Cut Probability Next Meeting: [X%] | Year-End Terminal Rate: [X%] - Liquidity/QT Status: [QT Active/Paused/Ended] | Balance Sheet: [size + trend] - Key Speaker Note: [name + tone + comment, or None] - Core Logic: [why this Fed stance + liquidity condition matters for US100 today] - US100 Policy Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

Fetch and report: US Macroeconomic Data (last 5 business days — confirmed released data only): - CPI (US Inflation) — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous. Beat or Miss? - PCE (Core Personal Consumption Expenditures) — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous. Beat or Miss? - GDP (US Growth) — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous. Beat or Miss? - NFP (Non-Farm Payrolls) — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous. Beat or Miss? Tech Sector Earnings (last 7 days — confirmed reports only): - List any "Magnificent 7" earnings reports: Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN), Meta (META), Tesla (TSLA), NVIDIA (NVDA) - List any major semiconductor earnings: NVDA, AMD, QCOM, AVGO, ASML - For each confirmed report: EPS Actual vs Estimate | Revenue Actual vs Estimate | Beat/Miss | Forward Guidance (raised/lowered/maintained) | Initial stock reaction - Any major AI/tech sector news or product announcements in the last 48 hours 
⚙ MARKET REGIME IDENTIFICATION (MANDATORY — output this before the compressed summary block): Based on the data you have collected in this phase AND the prevailing macro narrative, identify the current Market Regime from the following categories: (A) 'Inflation Fears' — the market is currently punishing hot economic data; bond yields are rising because of inflation, not growth; rate-cut expectations are falling; 'bad news is bad news' and 'good news is bad news' for risk assets. (B) 'Growth / Recession Fears' — the market is worried about economic slowdown or recession; weak data signals demand destruction, not rate relief; 'bad news is bad news' for both bonds and risk assets. (C) 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Optimism' — the market is in 'bad news is good news' mode; soft data raises rate-cut probabilities; risk assets rally on weak prints because easier policy is the dominant narrative. (D) 'Mixed / Transitional' — conflicting signals; the regime is unclear or shifting. IMPORTANT: The identified regime MUST be used to correctly interpret every data point in this phase. For example: in regime (C), a weak PMI print should be flagged as potentially BULLISH for risk assets (more cuts), NOT bearish. Output: - Current Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] - Regime Interpretation: [one sentence — how this regime changes the reading of today's data vs face value] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - US Inflation/Growth Trend: [Hot/Cooling/Mixed] | Key Data Beat/Miss: [top confirmed data point] - Mag 7 / Tech Earnings Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] — list confirmed earnings beats or misses - Top Macro Driver Today: [the single most impactful confirmed data point or earnings release] - US100 Momentum Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P3 - TECH SECTOR MOMENTUM & US MACRO] - US Inflation/Growth Trend: [Hot/Cooling/Mixed] | Key Driver: [confirmed data point + beat/miss] - Mag 7 / Semiconductor Earnings: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] | Top Release: [company + EPS/Rev + beat/miss + guidance] - AI/Tech Sector Catalyst: [key news or announcement, or None] - Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] | Regime Interpretation: [one line] 
- Core Logic: [how macro data + earnings momentum combine to drive US100 sentiment today] - US100 Momentum Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

phase6_master_bias:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Synthesize the 5 fundamental research inputs below into a clear US100 (NASDAQ 100) institutional intraday bias.

Research Inputs:
1. Fed Policy & Liquidity: [P1_OUTPUT]
2. Treasury Yields & Dollar: [P2_OUTPUT]
3. Tech Sector Momentum & US Macro: [P3_OUTPUT]
4. Sentiment & Volatility: [P4_OUTPUT]
5. Macro & Sector Risks: [P5_OUTPUT]

⚡ STEP 0 — DOMINANT THEME IDENTIFICATION & DYNAMIC WEIGHTING (MANDATORY — complete this before synthesising the 5 pillars): Identify the single Dominant Market Theme driving price action today. Choose the most accurate label: 'Tier-1 Macro Data Release (NFP/CPI/GDP/FOMC)', 'Central Bank Policy Shift', 'Safe-Haven / Risk-Off Flow Event', 'Geopolitical Escalation / De-escalation', 'Institutional Flow Surge (ETF/COT)', 'On-Chain / Tokenomics Catalyst', 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Repricing', 'Commodity Supply Shock', or 'No Clear Dominant Theme — Weight Equally'. WEIGHTING RULE: The pillar associated with the Dominant Theme should receive approximately 60% of the decision weight. The remaining 4 pillars share the remaining 40% equally (~10% each). OVERRIDE RULE: If a Tier-1 macro event (NFP, CPI, FOMC decision, or equivalent central-bank rate decision) has occurred today or is PENDING today, you MUST explicitly override and disregard short-term retail sentiment signals (IG Client Sentiment, CNN Fear & Greed) for the directional bias — these contrarian retail signals become statistically unreliable around high-impact macro events. State: 'Retail sentiment overridden — Tier-1 macro event active.' Output: - Dominant Theme: [theme label] - Dominant Pillar: [which of the 5 pillars maps to this theme, e.g., 'Pillar 3'] - Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60%] | Remaining Pillars: [~10% each] - Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No] 
🔄 STEP 1 — MARKET REGIME ALIGNMENT CHECK (MANDATORY): State the current Market Regime: ['Inflation Fears' / 'Growth/Recession Fears' / 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism' / 'Mixed']. Then verify that your final directional bias logically aligns with this regime: — In 'Inflation Fears': strong economic data = fewer cuts = bearish risk assets / bearish precious metals / bearish bonds. — In 'Growth/Recession Fears': weak data = demand destruction = bearish commodities and risk assets; strong data = relief rally. — In 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism': weak data = more cuts = bullish risk assets, crypto, and gold; treat 'bad news as good news'. If your bias contradicts the prevailing regime without a specific overriding catalyst, lower confidence by 1 point and explain the contradiction. Output: - Market Regime: [label] - Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts regime — reason] 
Instructions:
- Check for contradictions across the 5 inputs. If 2 or more major factors conflict, lower confidence by at least 2 points and state why.
- If all factors align, confirm alignment and justify higher confidence.
- Pay special attention to the relationship between Fed liquidity (P1), 10Y yields (P2), and tech valuations — these are the primary structural drivers of the US100.
- Recommend the best time-window to trade this bias today based on scheduled events, earnings releases, and session liquidity (US pre-market / NY open / mid-session).

Output (bullet points only, no long paragraphs):
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10] — adjusted down if contradictions found
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [top factor + why it dominates today]
- Primary Driver 2: [second factor + why it matters]
- Dominant Theme: [theme label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60% to dominant pillar | ~10% each to others]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No]
- Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts — reason]
- Contradiction Check: [list any conflicting signals, or "None — all factors aligned"]
- Invalidation Point: [what specific event or data shift would flip this bias]
- Optimal Trading Window: [e.g., NY open + time UTC + reason, or pre-market + key catalyst]

Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Steps 2.2 and 2.3):
```
[US100 MASTER BIAS - [CURRENT_DATE]]
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10]
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [factor + logic]
- Primary Driver 2: [factor + logic]
- Dominant Theme: [label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [dominant pillar + ~60%]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes / No]
- Market Regime: [label]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts]
- Contradiction: [summary or "None"]
- Invalidation: [specific event or level]
- Optimal Trading Window: [session + time UTC + reason]
```

phase8_blog_html: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Act as an expert Index Analyst and Financial Blogger. Using the research data below, write a comprehensive, highly detailed blog post explaining today's US100 (NASDAQ 100) fundamental bias so anyone reading it can fully understand the market conditions. Language Rule: Write the post in engaging Bengali (Bangla script), but you MUST keep all technical financial terms (e.g., Hawkish, Dovish, Fed, QT, Quantitative Tightening, Treasury Yield, Discount Rate, P/E Multiple, VIX, Bullish, Bearish, Confidence Level, Magnificent 7, Earnings Per Share, Beat, Miss, Guidance, Safe-Haven, Risk-On/Off, NFP, CPI, PCE, GDP, DXY, Put/Call Ratio, COT, Semiconductor, Export Ban) strictly in English.
